theme: Islamic star mosaic with mixed star orders
title: Star Lattice Interfaces
seed_problem: planar patch of tiles assigned star motifs under a fixed edge-angle geometry
mutation: composite: cells come in two kinds -- STAR cells restricted to a small fixed order palette, and interstitial ADAPTER cells free to use any order dividing the global angle modulus at a real cost -- coupling a mixed-rotational-order diversity/alignment bonus to a cost-budgeted adapter placement that must number-theoretically reconcile two zones' incompatible fixed angles; the interface language between star zones, not the stars themselves, becomes the design problem.
strategies: trivial: build every star cell with a single fixed palette order, never build any adapter (reproduces the checker baseline)
naive greedy: keep the single fixed star order, but blindly build every adapter with the universal (most expensive) order regardless of how much cheaper a sufficient order would have been

insight (strong): per star cell, pick the order that first maximizes the canonical-phase bonus and second favors an order used least so far (maximizes order diversity for free); per adapter, compute the number-theoretically minimal sufficient order via gcd of its incident angle differences and only build when the strand-length payoff covers that (now minimal) cost

a general solver can go further: joint global assignment of star orders (e.g. weighted bipartite matching against palette slots) and a knapsack over which adapters to afford under a shared cost/length trade-off
